title_001: The Energy Bus Master Quiz — The Night Library
html_005: I am the driver of my life’s bus
html_007: “The one who drives the bus of your life
html_009: — The Energy Bus, the very first rule
html_010: Starting from a flat-tire morning, master the life-changing
html_014:
.
        It starts easy and gets harder step by step,
html_015: and any question you get wrong comes back until you nail it.
html_032: That’s the end of the free preview
html_045: The Energy Bus at a glance
html_048: into one and you’ll never forget it. The starting point of everything is
html_052: 🛞 The flat-tire morning — George in crisis
html_053: A double crisis shaking both team and home
html_055: 🕹️ Take the wheel — I’m the driver (Rules 1·2)
html_056: Vision and focus set the direction
html_059: E+P=O · don’t waste energy on those who won’t board
html_061: 🧛 Block the vampires, ❤️ lead with love (Rules 6–8)
html_062: Protect the culture, lead with love instead of fear
html_064: 🎯 Drive with purpose, enjoy the ride, arrive (Rules 9·10)
html_065: One united team — and a life running again
html_069: 3 cases from the book worth pondering
html_071: Focused review of what you missed
html_073: you can watch Jon Gordon share his positive-energy story firsthand.
html_079: Dopamine-fueled repeat-master quizzes that lock in a book’s core
html_080: This app is secondary learning content that reconstructs the book’s core concepts for study purposes. For the full reading experience, please meet the original book itself.

js_004: What was the direct reason George started riding the bus every day?
js_005: His car tire went flat on Monday morning
js_006: The company recalled every executive car
js_007: His license was suspended for drunk driving
js_008: He chose public transit to help the environment
js_009: Every story begins with a flat tire. The morning that looked like the worst becomes the greatest stroke of luck in George’s life. It’s the whole book foreshadowing that crisis is the doorway to opportunity.
js_011: Who drives the No.11 bus George boards — the mentor of this book?
js_012: Joy, the driver who always smiles and shares each rule
js_013: Martha, who drove in silence and handed over a letter on the last day
js_014: The white-haired old professor who mentored George in college
js_015: A retired psychologist, an old man sitting in the next seat
js_016: The driver Joy is exactly what the name says — joy. Over two weeks, Joy and the passengers pass George the 10 Rules one at a time.
js_018: The first rule — who should be the driver of the bus that is my life?
js_020: The family who cares for me most
js_022: The times and luck that read the flow
js_023: “I am the driver of my bus.” — the moment you hand the wheel to circumstances or other people, the bus heads to someone else’s destination. It’s the premise behind every rule.
js_025: In this book, what does the ‘bus’ best symbolize?
js_026: My life, and the work and team I lead
js_027: A company’s org chart and hierarchy that must run without a hitch
js_028: Public transit that everyone uses equally
js_029: A fate that only runs the one fixed route
js_030: The bus = the journey of my life and the team I’m responsible for. That’s why who you pick up, what fuel you run on, and where you drive are all your choice.
js_032: What is the ‘fuel’ Joy told George to fill the bus with?
js_037: The bus’s fuel is positive energy. Not talent or plans — energy decides the journey. Negative thoughts are like punching a hole in the fuel tank.
js_039: Around the time he was riding the bus, what was George’s situation at work?
js_040: The new NRG-2000 launch is two weeks away and the team is about to fall apart
js_041: He’d just passed a promotion review, thrilled to move to a new department
js_042: The company had gone bankrupt and next month’s paycheck was about to stop
js_043: He was mulling over a scout offer from a rival company
js_044: George, team lead at the lighting company NRG, was facing a product launch two weeks away with both his team and his home falling apart — a double crisis. The stage was already set to put what he learned on the bus into real practice.
js_046: The sign Joy told him to post on the bus — who is barred from boarding?
js_051: Energy Vampires — people who drain others’ energy with complaints, cynicism, and blame. The sign “No Energy Vampires on my bus” is the boundary that guards your energy.
js_053: What’s the right way to get people onto your bus?
js_054: Share your vision and invite them to come along
js_055: Offer raises and promotions and have them sign a contract
js_056: Quietly draw up a list and notify them
js_057: Make them compete and take only the winners
js_058: People move through invitation, not force. George handed his team bus tickets and shared his vision. Leaving whether to board as the other person’s choice is part of the invitation.
js_060: About how long did George commute on Joy’s bus?
js_061: About two weeks, while his car was being repaired
js_062: About 100 days, after finishing his training
js_063: About six months, while preparing to switch jobs
js_064: About a year, after the project wrapped up
js_065: Exactly two weeks. Yet that short commute changes his whole life. It’s an allegorical device, designed so the bus journey overlaps with the time left until launch day.
js_067: What was the final, tenth rule Joy passed on?
js_068: Enjoy the ride while you’re on the bus
js_069: Name a successor before you get off the bus
js_070: Keep switching to a bigger, faster bus
js_071: When you reach the last stop, go back to the start
js_072: Watch only the destination and you miss the journey. “Enjoy the ride while you’re on it” — the bus of life is one-way anyway, so enjoying it is part of the point.
js_074: Jon Gordon official · The Energy Bus
js_077: The flat tire is this fable’s ‘invitation.’ It’s also the first practice case for the E+P=O formula (question 11).
js_078: The name Joy is itself a spoiler — that joy is the teacher.
js_079: Rule 1 is the premise. Start by examining the habit of handing the driver’s seat to others (blaming people, blaming circumstances).
js_080: The bus is a person’s own life and, at the same time, keeps expanding into a metaphor for the team and home a leader guides.
js_081: Positive energy isn’t a mood but fuel — the point is that it’s the force that actually gets your vision moving.
js_082: Remember the story’s structure, built so launch D-14 overlaps with the two-week bus ride, and the whole flow clicks into place.
js_083: The vampire isn’t a villain but ‘a pattern that drains energy.’ The sign bans a behavior, not a person.
js_084: The opposite of invitation is coercion. Hand over the ticket but leave the choice to them — that’s the whole of Rule 4.
js_085: The short two-week setup is also a message: ‘energy spreads fast.’
js_086: Rule 10 isn’t a bonus but the conclusion — if you can’t enjoy the journey, the destination loses its meaning too.
js_101: The wheel of the bus — life, work, team — is in my hands. Blaming others and circumstances just leaves the driver’s seat empty.
js_103: Desire, vision, and focus set the bus’s direction. We move toward what we focus on.
js_105: Energy, more than talent, decides the journey. Gratitude is the cheapest, surest fuel.
js_107: Share the vision and invite them aboard. Whether they board is left to their choice.
js_108: A pattern that drains energy with complaints and cynicism. Give a chance, but if it harms the culture, let them off.
js_109: Event (E) + Perception (P) = Outcome (O). You can’t change the event, but you always choose the interpretation.
js_111: Make time, listen, acknowledge, serve, bring out their best. Not fear but love builds a team that lasts.
js_112: Regardless of title, the ultimate owner of the team’s energy. Drive with purpose and the work becomes a calling.
js_115: On Monday morning, George’s car tire goes flat. His life, too, is already on the edge of a blowout.
js_116: George, team lead at NRG — the NRG-2000 launch is two weeks off, the team is near collapse, and home is shaky too.
js_117: He reluctantly boards the No.11 bus. The driver, Joy, greets him with a bright smile.
js_118: Joy’s declaration — “You didn’t board this bus by accident.” The two weeks of lessons begin.
js_121: Rule 1 — I am the driver of my bus. Don’t hand the wheel to anyone else.
js_122: Rule 2 — desire, vision, and focus set the direction. Picture what you want every day.
js_123: Rule 3 — fill up on positive energy as fuel. Negative thoughts are a hole in the tank.
js_124: E+P=O — the perception you add to an event makes the outcome. Even a flat tire can turn into luck.
js_125: The gratitude walk — just recalling things to be grateful for as you walk tops up the fuel.
js_128: Rule 4 — invite people onto the bus and share the vision. George hands his team members tickets.
js_129: The essence of invitation — not force, but leaving the choice to the other person.
js_130: Rule 5 — don’t waste energy on those who won’t board. You can’t take everyone.
js_131: As you keep going, someone who didn’t board at first may hop on at the next stop.
js_134: Rule 6 — post the sign “No Energy Vampires on board.”
js_135: The vampire is a pattern, not a person — complaints, cynicism, and blame drain the team’s fuel.
js_136: George in practice — he states expectations clearly, offers a chance to change, and lets go the members who refuse.
js_137: The point of firmness isn’t punishment but protecting the energy of the remaining passengers.
js_140: Rule 7 — captivate passengers with passion. The root of ‘enthusiasm’ is entheos, the god within.
js_141: Rule 8 — love your passengers. In five ways: give time, listen, acknowledge, serve, bring out their best.
js_142: George applies the same rules at home too — the closest passengers are family.
js_143: Rule 9 — drive with purpose. With purpose, even the same work becomes a calling and never wears you out.
js_144: George’s redefinition — he decides to be not a team lead but a Chief Energy Officer.
js_146: The destination, and enjoying it · Rule 10
js_147: Launch day — the now-united team makes the NRG-2000 launch a success.
js_148: Rule 10 — enjoy the ride while you’re on the bus. The bus of life is one-way.
js_149: George now becomes the driver who takes his own team and family onto his bus.
js_150: Your turn — the book’s last page is a departure notice for the reader’s own bus.
js_151: The morning a flat tire became luck
js_152: Had the car not broken down, George would never have met Joy. The classic scene where the worst event becomes the best turning point.
js_153: E+P=O — because it’s the starting point for practicing how to change your interpretation when you can’t change the event.
js_154: The bus tickets he handed his team
js_155: Two weeks before launch, George shares his vision with his team and hands out tickets — leaving boarding, or not, as each person’s own choice.
js_156: The line between invitation and coercion — because voluntary energy only arises when you give people the choice.
js_157: The vampire who wouldn’t change, getting off
js_158: He gave them a chance, but a member who never dropped the cynicism is asked by George to get off the bus. His hardest decision.
js_159: Because it makes you think about why a leader’s firmness is a protection for the remaining passengers.
js_160: Right now, who’s holding the wheel of my life’s bus?
js_161: If, over the past month, you made important decisions ‘because you had no choice,’ look back at who you handed the driver’s seat to.
js_162: Who are the energy vampires around me, and how am I dealing with them?
js_163: And flip it around — whether I’ve ever been the vampire on someone else’s bus, too.
js_164: What purpose am I driving toward?
js_165: If work feels like drain, it’s time to check meaning before volume. Write down who your work makes smile.
js_167: You saw right through all 10 rules
js_169: You’ve grabbed almost all the core rules
js_171: You’ve got the framework — one more time!
js_173: The more you repeat, the higher your score climbs

_app.title: The Energy Bus Master Quiz
_app.desc: From a flat-tire morning, 10 life-changing rules — a 30-question quiz and a Reading Report.
